page: Amazon Web Services z1d.xlarge is a high-frequency, memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8151 processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering a sustained core frequency of 4.0 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 vCPUs derived from 2 dedicated physical cores supporting hyper-threading. It includes 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 150 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reflect a strong single-core profile balanced by moderate multi-core capabilities. The high-frequency CPU achieves average tier results in single-core stress-ng (1652.79) and Geekbench single-core (1314.0) tests. Single-threaded operations like gzip compression (28.05 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (10.76 GB/sec) also perform in the average range. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the weak to poor categories, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 2868.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 20.29 GB/sec. Large block memory read and write speeds are measured at 27.56 GB/sec and 24.07 GB/sec respectively.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the z1d.xlarge offers a high-frequency compute capability paired with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio (1:8) and fast local NVMe storage, making it a specialized option for workloads that are sensitive to per-core licensing fees or require high single-threaded performance. The instance is suitable for relational database workloads, electronic design automation (EDA), and memory-intensive applications that do not require massive parallel scaling but benefit from high clock speeds and local SSD scratch space.
description: The Amazon Web Services z1d.xlarge is a high-frequency, memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8151 x86_64 processor running at 4.0 GHz, configured with 4 vCPUs (2 physical cores), 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a bundled 150 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. While single-core benchmarks like Geekbench (1314.0) and single-threaded gzip compression (28.05 GB/sec) show average performance, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall into the weaker tiers. Qualitatively, this instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and high clock speeds, making it cost-effective for workloads with high per-core licensing costs. It is suitable for relational databases, electronic design automation, and applications requiring strong single-threaded performance paired with fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services z1d.xlarge is a high-frequency instance featuring a 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 4 vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 150 GB local NVMe SSD, optimized for high single-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services z1d.xlarge features a 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 32 GB RAM, and 150 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for memory-intensive, high-frequency workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated high-clock CPU cores and fast local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8151 processor running at a high sustained 4.0 GHz clock speed.
32.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
Bundled 150 GB NVMe SSD local instance store for low-latency scratch space.
Average single-core performance suitable for high per-core licensing workloads.
Best suited for relational databases, EDA, and single-threaded applications.
